title: You Thought I Was That Type
content:
You thought I was that type:
That you could forget me,
And that I'd plead and weep
And throw myself under the hooves of a bay mare,

Or that I'd ask the sorcerers
For some magic potion made from roots and send you a terrible gift:
My precious perfumed handkerchief.

Damn you! I will not grant your cursed soul
Vicarious tears or a single glance.

And I swear to you by the garden of the angels,
I swear by the miracle-working icon,
And by the fire and smoke of our nights:
I will never come back to you.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Anna Akhmatova delivers a powerful performance in "You Thought I Was That Type"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
